digest_queue.title: Ames Science Stars of the Month May 2026
digest_queue.resumo_pt: Resumo (PT-BR): 2 min read Ames Science Stars of the Month May 2026 Aaron McKinnon May 06, 2026 Article NASA Ames Science Directorate Stars of the Month: May 2026 The NASA Ames Science Directorate recognizes the outstanding contributions of (pictured left to right) Lora Jovanović, Tammy Moore, Frances Donovan, and Jaden Ta. Their commitment to the NASA mission represents the entrepreneurial spirit, technical expertise, and collaborative disposition needed to explore this world and beyond.

digest_queue.title: NASA’s Dryden Aeronautical Test Range Supports Flight, Space Missions
digest_queue.resumo_pt: Resumo (PT-BR): 3 min read NASA’s Dryden Aeronautical Test Range Supports Flight, Space Missions Jay Levine NASA Armstrong Public Affairs Specialist May 06, 2026 Article Contents Preparing for future flights Supporting space station operations Advancing NASA's mission Range operators at the Dryden Aeronautical Test Range at NASA’s Armstong Flight Research Center in Edwards, California, provide voice and tracking support to the International Space Station. 6, 2025, photo, Alex Oganesyan, left, and Deming Ingles are at their workstations, where they support communications backup for space station missions.

digest_queue.title: Rescue mission for NASA’s $500 million space telescope passes key testing milestone
digest_queue.resumo_pt: Resumo (PT-BR): Rescue mission for NASA’s $500 million space telescope passes key testing milestone May 8, 2026 Will Robinson-Smith Engineers from Katalyst stabilize their Link robotic servicing spacecraft as it moves into a vibration chamber at NASA Goddard on April 15, 2026. The vibration chamber simulated the intense shaking Link will experience during launch.

digest_queue.resumo_pt: Resumo (PT-BR): Photojournal Navigation Science Photojournal NASA’s Curiosity Takes Close... Photojournal Home Photojournal Search Latest Content Galleries Feedback RSS About 2 Min Read NASA’s Curiosity Takes Close Look at Rock That Got Stuck on Drill PIA26724 Credits: NASA/JPL-Caltech/MSSS Image Addition Date: 05/11/2026 Downloads NASA's Curiosity Takes Close Look at Rock That Got Stuck on Drill PNG (20.94 MB) Description NASA’s Curiosity Mars rover used its Mast Camera, or Mastcam, to capture this view of a rock nicknamed “Atacama” on May 6, 2026, the 4,877th Martian day, or sol, of the mission.
